what_it_does: This file is the module constitution for the file-discovered relation-type registry: it defines what a relation-type is, the required RELATION_TYPE dict schema, the live set of edge kinds, and the rules for adding and maintaining relation types.
observations: Relation-types are file-discovered, one self-registering relation_types/<id>.py per kind, mirroring roles/skills/projections/node-types registries.
Each relation-type declares a module-level RELATION_TYPE dict with schema fields id/directed/inverse/near/far/label/desc.
The id must equal the file stem; malformed entries fail loudly; non-RELATION_TYPE or _ files are skipped.
It enumerates the live relation types: principle_beneath, fragment_of, contradicts, sibling.
It includes agent-authored entries depends_on and precedes, appended with an HTML comment instructing auto-reflection.
It enforces a render-not-judge floor: relation-types describe edges, judgement is a later pass.
It identifies a seam: discovered by runtime/relation_types.py and consumed by find_relations, with wiring as a separate coordinated pass.
Acceptance tests assert every relation type is reflected in this file.
standouts: The add-a-row-is-a-file registry design, avoiding a central Python dict.
Relation-types are declared data/vocabulary, not actions, with a render-not-judge floor.
The file doubles as the canonical catalog that acceptance tests check against the filesystem.
conventions: Constitution-style YAML frontmatter with type, register, module, aliases, tags, governs, relates-to, status.
File-discovered registry pattern consistent with other company registries.
Schema-enforced declarations with loud failure on malformed entries.
Manual synchronization between filesystem entries and this prose catalog, enforced by acceptance tests.
Agent-authored entries appended with an HTML comment process marker.
concerns: Acceptance tests require this prose list to stay in sync with filesystem files, creating manual drift risk.
Agent-authored entries mix an HTML comment instruction into the visible constitution text.
The mapping from near/far projection spaces to actual projection modules is referenced but not specified here.
The create_relation_type seam is flagged as a future separate coordinated pass, indicating incomplete wiring.
notes: This is the constitution and drift-home catalog for the file-discovered relation-type vocabulary, listing each edge kind and the rules for adding new ones.
questions: How exactly does runtime/relation_types.py discover files and enforce id == file stem?
How does find_relations translate near/far projection-space names into query_index calls?
What is the intended lifecycle for agent-authored entries relative to manually authored relation types?
What specific assertions do the acceptance tests make, and where are they located?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it defines the registry mechanics and enumerates the live relation types, though the agent-authored entries are appended rather than integrated into the main enumerated list.
apparent_themes: Living constitution maintained by acceptance tests.
Vocabulary as a substrate for cognition queries.
intention_signals: It aims to create a minimal, extensible edge vocabulary for corpus relations that can grow by dropping in files without editing central code, while keeping a human-readable canonical catalog that tests can verify.
novelty: It treats relation-types as a directory-discovered registry on par with roles/skills/projections, with each edge kind declared as a file rather than a central dictionary literal.
contribution: The relation-type schema, the enumerated live set of edge kinds, and the rule that new edge kinds are added as files and reflected in this constitution.
summary_for_embedding: The module constitution for the file-discovered relation-type registry, defining the RELATION_TYPE schema, listing the live directed and symmetric edge kinds, and prescribing how to add new relation types as files.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a stable, extensible vocabulary of typed corpus edges that find_relations can label without hardcoding edge kinds.
